summary: Comprehensive testing of new Audit Log viewer replacing broken System Logs section. All 25 backend tests passed (100%) and all 15 frontend UI tests passed. Backend: /api/audit-logs with all filters (success, event_type, symbol, broker_id), multi-event_type $in query, pagination (limit/skip), and /api/audit-logs/event-types all work correctly. Frontend: Audit Log section displays correctly with event count, category pills, symbol/broker inputs, status filter, auto-refresh, expandable rows with JSON details, and Loss Trade Files collapsible section all working as expected.

ui_bugs.issue: React DOM nesting warning: <button> (refresh-loss-logs-btn) nested inside <button> (loss-logs-toggle). Triggers 'validateDOMNesting' console warning. Functionally works but is invalid HTML.

action_items: LOW: Fix nested <button> inside <button> in LossLogViewer — change inner refresh button to a <div> or <span> with onClick to fix DOM nesting violation (line ~452 in LogsTab.tsx)
critical_code_review_comments: LossLogViewer has nested <button> inside <button>: The refresh icon button (data-testid='refresh-loss-logs-btn') is rendered inside the outer toggle <button> (loss-logs-toggle). React warns 'validateDOMNesting: <button> cannot appear as a descendant of <button>'. Fix: wrap the refresh button in a <span role='button'> or restructure to avoid nesting.

Frontend client-side category filtering is a fallback (comment in code says 'backend supports single event_type only') but the backend actually supports multi event_type via $in query. The frontend could be simplified to let the backend do all filtering — but this is not a bug, just an opportunity for optimization.

AuditLogViewer buildParams() is built but never actually used — the url construction on line 189 re-builds the URL manually instead of using buildParams(). Dead code exists at lines 167-178. Not a bug but messy.
seed_data_creation: Triggered 2 SETTING_CHANGED audit events via /api/price-sources/toggle during test_setting_change_creates_audit_log (toggled and restored). These appear in the DB audit_logs collection.

frontend_ui_tests.category_pills_all_6: PASS — All, Trading, Broker, Config, Engine, System all visible
frontend_ui_tests.audit_row_display: PASS — 30 rows shown with timestamp, event_type badge, success icon
frontend_ui_tests.row_expand_json_details: PASS — clicking row shows JSON details (e.g. prefer_broker_feeds SETTING_CHANGED)
frontend_ui_tests.fail_filter: PASS — FAIL filter shows 0 failed events (all current logs are success=true)
frontend_ui_tests.config_category_filter: PASS — Config pill shows 23 SETTING_CHANGED/TICKER events
frontend_ui_tests.loss_files_expand_on_click: PASS — expanded showing 51 files across 5 dates

context_for_next_testing_agent: Audit Log viewer feature is fully functional. Backend: 26 event types, all filters working (success, event_type with $in, symbol, broker_id, pagination). Frontend: Full UI working — category pills, status filter, auto-refresh, expandable rows with JSON, Loss Trade Files collapsible. Only minor issue: nested <button> DOM nesting in LossLogViewer refresh button. DB has 30 audit log entries (all SETTING_CHANGED from previous tests). Loss Trade Files has 51 files across 5 dates (2026-03-17 to 2026-03-26).
rca_of_issue: Nested button DOM warning: LossLogViewer renders a <button data-testid='refresh-loss-logs-btn'> as a child of the outer toggle <button data-testid='loss-logs-toggle'>. HTML spec prohibits interactive elements nested inside buttons. Fix: convert inner element to <span role='button'> or restructure the flex container to sit outside the outer button.
